a260719p1.summary: While conducting espionage against powerful sheikh's tech company, Jonny Gannon helped Gulf country quash Washington suspicions about China ties
a260719p1.title: Sources: CIA operative Jonny Gannon spied on G42 to probe its China ties and helped the UAE gain expanded access to US AI chips by allaying US suspicions (Wall Street Journal)

a260719p2.summary: Large corporate chains lose their edge as services such as DoorDash and Uber Eats level the playing field
a260719p2.title: Big US pizza delivery chains are struggling as apps such as DoorDash and Uber Eats give independent pizzerias and other restaurants greater market access (Haley Zimmerman/Financial Times)

a260719p3.summary: Sebastian Rucci's past has become a theme in opposition to his proposal. He counters: 'Do I look like a guy that's going away?'
a260719p3.title: A profile of businessman Sebastian Rucci, whose past ventures were subject to legal probes, as he bids to build California's biggest data center, a $10B project (Zusha Elinson/Wall Street Journal)

a260719p4.summary: San Francisco is still waiting for the AI IPOs — the wave of money from Anthropic and OpenAI millionaires and billionaires that is likely …
a260719p4.title: Analysis: OpenAI and Anthropic employees are donating to campaigns more heavily and cohesively than Google, Meta, and Airbnb employees did post-IPO (Alexandra Lindsay/The San Francisco ...)

a260719p5.summary: Moonshot AI has told investors it's preparing to list in as early as six months, seizing the opportunity to tap capital markets …
a260719p5.title: Sources: Kimi developer Moonshot told investors it is preparing for a Hong Kong IPO in as early as six months; its ARR hit $300M in June, up from $200M in April (Bloomberg)

a260719p6.summary: Here are the ways leading companies are already responding to the AI revolution in professional services
a260719p6.title: AI is reshaping entry-level professional services jobs, as companies redesign hiring, training, and workplace culture rather than simply cut junior roles (Andrew Hill/Financial Times)
